have h1 : (rewardByCount a (n + 1)) ⟂ᵢ[ Bandit.measure alg ν] (fun ω (i : Iic n) ↦ rewardByCount a (↑i) ω) := by
    -- Use the fact that the reward at step (n+1) is independent of the rewards at previous steps

      intro k hk

      have h3 : (rewardByCount a (n + 1)) ⟂ᵢ[ Bandit.measure alg ν] (fun ω ↦ rewardByCount a k ω) := by
        -- Prove that the reward at step (n+1) is independent of the reward at step k

          -- Use the properties of the measure and the algorithm to establish independence

            -- Prove that the sets are independent

            exact h6
          -- Use the independence of the sets to establish the independence of the functions
          have h7 : (rewardByCount a (n + 1)) ⟂ᵢ[ Bandit.measure alg ν] (fun ω ↦ rewardByCount a k ω) := by
            -- Use the properties of the measure and the algorithm to establish independence
            sorry
          exact h7
        exact h4
      exact h3
    
    -- Use the independence of the reward at step (n+1) with all previous rewards to establish the overall independence
    have h3 : (rewardByCount a (n + 1)) ⟂ᵢ[ Bandit.measure alg ν] (fun ω (i : Iic n) ↦ rewardByCount a (↑i) ω) := by
      -- Prove that the reward at step (n+1) is independent of the function mapping i to rewardByCount a i
      have h4 : (rewardByCount a (n + 1)) ⟂ᵢ[ Bandit.measure alg ν] (fun ω (i : Iic n) ↦ rewardByCount a (↑i) ω) := by
        -- Use the properties of the measure and the algorithm to establish independence
        have h5 : (rewardByCount a (n + 1)) ⟂ᵢ[ Bandit.measure alg ν] (fun ω (i : Iic n) ↦ rewardByCount a (↑i) ω) := by
